title: Guzmania pearcei
taxonomy: Guzmania pearcei is a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. Guzmania pearcei is classified as a type of epiphyte, which is a plant that grows on other plants, such as trees, but does not derive nutrients from them. This species is native to Central and South America, and it is a member of the genus Guzmania, which contains around 120 species of plants. Guzmania pearcei is a popular ornamental plant due to its unique and attractive appearance, featuring long, narrow leaves and brightly colored flowers. The exact classification of Guzmania pearcei at the rank of genus and species is Guzmania pearcei.

intro: Guzmania pearcei, commonly known as the Pearce's airplant or Pearce's bromeliad, is a terrestrial epiphyte belonging to the family Bromeliaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and research has identified its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation, fight infections, and protect against cell damage.
